task_completion.description: The agent accessed the correct patient record in the Bahmni Clinical system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni Clinical dashboard or patient summary is open, clearly displaying the patient name 'Mohammed Al-Rashidi' and identifier 'BAH000022'.

task_completion.description: The agent documented the patient's vitals for the active OPD visit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vitals or Observations tab shows newly entered physiological measurements (e.g., blood pressure, heart rate, weight) saved for the current visit.

task_completion.description: The agent recorded coded clinical diagnoses for both chronic conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Diagnoses section lists both 'Type 2 Diabetes mellitus' (or similar coded diabetes diagnosis) and 'Essential Hypertension' as active or confirmed diagnoses.

task_completion.description: The agent prescribed at least one appropriate medication for each of the two conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Medications or Treatment section shows active prescriptions for at least one anti-diabetic medication (e.g., Metformin, Insulin) and at least one anti-hypertensive medication (e.g., Lisinopril, Amlodipine).

task_completion.description: The agent provided a clinical note summarizing the findings and management plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Consultation Notes or Observation text area contains a typed summary detailing the clinical findings and the management plan for the patient.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the Bahmni EMR GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Bahmni web interface (https://localhost/bahmni/home) rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the actual EMR forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into the proper Bahmni UI fields, dropdowns, and text boxes, rather than being typed into a separate text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent performed all actions on the correct patient's record.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient header consistently displays 'Mohammed Al-Rashidi' and 'BAH000022' across the different tabs (Vitals, Diagnoses, Medications, Notes) where data is entered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Mohammed Al-Rashidi. Patient Identifier: BAH000022. The agent must interact with this specific patient's record in the Bahmni EMR. The required diagnoses to be recorded are Type 2 Diabetes mellitus and Essential Hypertension. The agent must prescribe at least one medication for each condition and write a clinical note.
